courses.description: \\Concurrent Enrollment Credit: FIN 1060 (with approved teacher): Mr Cook or Mr Boyd Are you ready to take control of your financial future? This course is designed to empower high schoolers with the skills they need to make informed financial decisions that will impact their lives now and in the future. In this hands-on and engaging class, you’ll learn how to: Be a smart consumer, saver, and investor. Manage credit responsibly and avoid common financial pitfalls. Plan and manage your personal budget like a pro. Explore short- and long-term savings and investment strategies. Understand the real-world impact of financial decisions on individuals and society. Set and achieve your personal financial goals. By the end of this course, you’ll have the tools and knowledge to build a solid financial foundation, ensuring you’re prepared for life’s opportunities and challenges. With concurrent enrollment options, this class also offers a chance to earn college credit while learning essential life skills. Take the first step toward financial independence and success—this is more than just a class; it’s a game-changer for your future! Optional college credit available with approved teacher for FIN 1060 with registration through UVU.

Learn an interactive, fun, and visual language, deep rooted in American Deaf Culture. This course will teach essential skills to communicate with those in the American Deaf Community. Learn over 700+ vocabulary words, ASL grammar, Deaf culture, and the skills you need to use the language. Build confidence while we learn and practice through enriching lessons, games, assignments and activities. In person attendance is required. Regular attendance allows the student to learn essential, in-class instruction, practice with peers, and allows the instructor to provide immediate feedback on sign production. This course is accepted at all major colleges and universities.

This course is designed to teach functional ASL which can be used in everyday interactions. Learning these functions will enable students to establish and maintain social relationships with others who use ASL. The grammar and vocabulary of ASL will be learned within the context of communicative activities. Students will be taught basic awareness of the Deaf culture through role-play situations, assigned readings, interactions with Deaf individuals and videotapes. Attendance will be mandatory. Facilitators manage the room and assignments while the instructor is broadcast over TV with course material.

In ASL 2 you will continue to learn an interacting, fun, and visual language, deep rooted in American Deaf Culture, as you move from novice to intermediate learner. This course will teach intermediate skills to communicate with those in the American Deaf Community. Continue to learn an additional 800+ vocabulary words, ASL grammar, Deaf culture, and the skills you need to use the language. Grow in confidence while we learn and practice through enriching lessons, games, assignments and activities. In person attendance is required. Regular attendance allows the student to learn essential, in-class instruction, practice with peers, and allows the instructor to provide immediate feedback on sign production. This course is accepted at all major colleges and universities.

Step into the world of French and become a global citizen! In this course, students explore the language and culture of French-speaking countries while building fundamental skills in speaking, listening, reading and writing that will help you communicate confidently. We’ll work through five units: Introductions, Physical and Personality Descriptions, Pastimes, School and Family, using interactive lessons, activities, and real-life scenarios. Communication in class (teacher-student and student-student) is primarily in French.

Students deepen their global view and expand their fluency in the French language as we work through five new units: Clothing and Weather, Home and Daily Routine, Healthy Eating, Hobbies and Interests, and Their Town. Students will increase their confidence in communication as we continue to explore the cultures of the French-speaking world in new real-life contexts. Communication in class (teacher-student and/or student-student) is primarily in French.

courses.description: Get ready to master French and take on the AP French Language & Culture Exam in May! This course challenges you to strengthen your reading, writing, listening, and speaking skills through real-world topics like family life, identity, art, science, technology, contemporary life, and global issues. You'll analyze texts, respond to emails, write persuasive essays, engage in conversations, and give cultural presentations—all in French. By the end of the year, you'll be confident in expressing opinions, telling detailed stories, and discussing a variety of topics using connected sentences and short paragraphs. If you're serious about French and want to earn college credit, this is the course for you!

Fees: Undoukai (Japanese Sports Festival)- $5 - transportation cost for bus per student, $5-10 - food trucks BYU Language Festival- $5 - transportation cost for bus per student, $5-10 - food and miscellaneous This class is taught in a facilitator-run classroom with a remote teacher. Japanese 1 is an introductory class on the language and culture of Japan. In this class the students will learn basic communication skills in Japanese through learning hiragana, katakana, greeting and phrases, and basic grammar and sentence structure.You will be taught to listen, speak, read, and write for simple situations and conversations. To pass this class the students must achieve mastery in hiragana and katakana. The students will be able to communicate in Japanese on a basic level. At the end of the course the students will be able to use and respond to basic greetings.

Fees: Undoukai (Japanese Sports Festival) $5 - transportation cost for bus per student, $5-10 - food trucks BYU Language Festival- $5 - transportation cost for bus per student, $5-10 - food and miscellaneous This class is taught in a facilitator-run classroom with a remote teacher. Japanese 2 is a continuation of Japanese 1\. In this class, the students will build upon the skills acquired in Japanese 1\. The students will be expected to master katakana and hiragana and increase their knowledge of kanji by increasing the number and difficulty of kanji learned. In Japanese 2, the students will begin to communicate in Japanese without using formulated sentence patterns. They will be able to take the skills learned in this class to participate in normal everyday activities.

Welcome to an epic journey through the Spanish-speaking world! This full-year adventure is designed to equip you with the skills to understand and speak Spanish like a pro. You'll discover how to navigate basic conversations with ease and gain confidence in your speaking abilities. But that's just the start! We’ll also unlock the mysteries of Spanish grammar and written communication, giving you a solid foundation for the language. Imagine being able to read and write, not just talk! And because language and culture go hand in hand, we'll explore the vibrant cultures and customs of Spanish-speaking countries. From the spicy rhythms of salsa dancing to the rich history of ancient civilizations, this course offers a deep dive into the diverse worlds of Spain, Mexico, and beyond. So, if you’re ready to expand your horizons and embrace a new way to communicate and connect, Spanish 1 is your ticket to a world of possibilities. Get ready to immerse yourself, engage with fascinating cultures, and have a lot of fun along the way! ![🌎][image1]![🎉][image2]

courses.description: The primary purpose of this course is to prepare students to pass the AP Spanish Language and Culture Exam. It covers six thematic units: Families in Different Societies, Language and Culture's Influence on Identity, Beauty and Art, Science and Technology, Quality of Life, and Environmental and Political Challenges. Students will develop advanced skills in interpreting texts, writing essays, and participating in conversations, with lessons conducted primarily in Spanish to enhance communication skills. Students can earn additional college credits based on their performance on the AP test, with transfer credit information available at BYU AP Credit Transfer and UVU AP Credit Transfer. If planning to attend a college other than BYU or UVU, students should check with that institution to determine AP transfer credit policies. Additionally, students will collaborate to create a film in Spanish, competing against other World Languages in a fun film festival!

Homework Support & Academic Catch-Up class offers a structured, supportive environment for students to focus on assignments, catch up on missed work, and receive the help they need to stay on track. This course is ideal for students with busy schedules, extracurricular activities, or those needing extra time and guidance to complete their assignments. Regular attendance is key to ensuring progress, as students will have the opportunity to work independently, receive one-on-one support from the instructor, and, if necessary, visit other teachers to work on missing assignments or projects. The class is pass/fail, with participation and consistent effort being the main criteria for success. This class is designed to help students stay on top of their academic responsibilities in a flexible, supportive setting.

NOTE: Only counselors can add this class with teacher recommendation. Empowers students through the four pillars of leadership, service, culture, and academics. We are dedicated to helping students reach their full potential while discovering joy and fulfillment through serving the Lehi community.. Among other things, students tutor at a local elementary school, visit an assisted living center, work with the Lehi Cares Coalition on community events, support school activities, and host Hispanic Heritage Week to celebrate Latino culture. Students also explore future careers, visit local universities, and develop the leadership skills needed to become active, impactful members of their community.

courses.description: NOTE: Only counselors can add this class with teacher recommendation. This course is built on the foundation of Sources of Strength, a nationally recognized, evidence-based program that moves "upstream" to promote mental health and prevent suicide. Rather than focusing on problems, we focus on prevention by cultivating eight key protective factors in our lives and our community. As a student-led group of young leaders, you will be trained to identify and amplify the Hope, Help, and Strength available to every Pioneer. You won’t just learn about resilience; you’ll lead it. This leadership role will include planning and running campaigns, which will require the occasional time spent outside of class helping at a lunchtime activity or a school event.

courses.description: Love singing, dancing, and acting? This high-energy course is for students who want to bring musical theatre to life! In this class, students will train in vocal performance, choreography, and acting techniques while preparing for the school’s fall and spring musical productions. Students will develop skills in auditioning, character work, and performance, working both in class and after school to create professional-quality productions. But this class is about more than just performing—it’s about building confidence, teamwork, communication, and discipline. Students will develop skills that go far beyond the stage, such as problem-solving, collaboration, time management, and resilience. Whether they go on to pursue a career in the arts or not, Musical Dance Theatre gives students the ability to express themselves, work effectively in a team, and present themselves with confidence in any setting. If you dream of performing or just want to challenge yourself in a supportive and creative environment, this class is the perfect opportunity! Contact Mindy Nelsen mnelsen@alpinedistrict.org
